stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 31% and 50%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $143K in good market conditions, $79K in bad market conditions. index fund: $104K in good market conditions, $22K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $203K in good market conditions, $50K in bad market conditions. real estate: $79K in good market conditions, $38K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.41: best option is tech stocks with EV=$112.7K. The optimal choice switches: bonds at P(good)=0.31, tech stocks at P(good)=0.5. EV range of best option: [98.8, 126.5].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: bonds at P(good)=0.31, tech stocks at P(good)=0.5.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
